channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: EFF alleges state abuse and cover-up in Phala Phala farm theft case
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) has accused President Cyril Ramaphosa of orchestrating a 'coordinated abuse of state power' in connection with the 2020 theft of foreign currency from his Phala Pha...

item.title: US Vice President JD Vance Heads to Pakistan for Sensitive Talks with Iran on Ceasefire and Nuclear Issues
item.description: Na so US Vice President JD Vance don warn Iran say make dem no try play America during the important talks wey go hold for Islamabad, Pakistan. Even though dem don agree on temporary ceasefire, plenty...

item.title: Murkomen Directs IG Kanja to Act on Political Goons Causing Chaos Ahead of Elections
item.description: Interior Cabinet Secretary Kipchumba Murkomen has called for a crackdown on political goons and private security groups used by politicians during elections. Speaking at a funeral in Kabarak, Murkomen...

item.title: Deanne Laubscher aims to challenge Gerda Steyn at the 2026 Two Oceans Marathon
item.description: Deanne Laubscher, representing Nedbank Running Club, is gearing up for the 2026 TotalSports Two Oceans Marathon with high hopes of challenging the six-time consecutive champion, Gerda Steyn, in the 56...

item.title: Roberts Takes First Jungle Shower on 'I'm a Celebrity'
item.description: Ashley Roberts, aged 44, made a splash on 'I'm a Celebrity... South Africa' as she became the first contestant to take a jungle shower. The former Pussycat Dolls singer's shower scene marked a signifi...

item.title: Dr. Ruto appoints Kepha Nyamweya Omae as nominee for NCIC chairmanship
item.description: President William Ruto has nominated Kepha Nyamweya Omae to chair the National Cohesion and Integration Commission (NCIC), an independent body charged with promoting national harmony and addressing et...

item.title: Babar Azam Breaks Record to Become Fastest to 12,000 T20 Runs, Surpassing Virat Kohli
item.description: Babar Azam, the captain of Pakistan's national team, made history by becoming the fastest player to reach 12,000 runs in T20 cricket. The record was achieved during a Pakistan Super League (PSL) match...

item.title: Zanzibar authorities investigate alleged genital theft reports, ten held for questioning
item.description: Police in Zanzibar have detained ten people following widespread claims that several men lost their genitalia under mysterious circumstances. The arrests were made after rumours spread in some neighbo...

item.title: Young Tanzanians take lead in building locally owned and authentic tourism ventures
item.description: Tanzania’s tourism sector has continued to show great strength even after facing major economic and political challenges. Despite the Covid-19 pandemic, high fuel costs, and post-election tensions, it...

item.title: Relive Iconic Gender-Bending Moments on 'Your Face Sounds Familiar'
item.description: ABS-CBN's 'Your Face Sounds Familiar' is known for its amazing transformations, and the gender-switch performances truly stand out. This article highlights twelve of the most memorable and impressive ...

item.title: Easing of Cold Treatment Regulations to Benefit South African Citrus Exports to China
item.description: South African citrus growers are celebrating a significant development as China eases cold treatment regulations for imports. Agriculture Minister John Steenhuisen has hailed the amendment as a positi...

item.title: ACT Wazalendo demands accountability and reforms for Tanzania's democracy
item.description: ACT Wazalendo, a prominent opposition party in Tanzania, has urged the Commonwealth to support the restoration of democratic governance in Tanzania, following the controversial 2025 General Election. ...

item.title: Calgary Stampede Annual Tarp Auction Raises Record $6.1 Million
item.description: The Calgary Stampede's annual tarp auction has achieved a historic milestone, attracting a record $6.1 million in bids this year. The event, which features decorated tarps used during the Stampede, ha...

item.title: Wike honours late Senator Barry Mpigi for loyalty and service
item.description: Nyesom Wike, Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, don talk say the late Senator Barinada Mpigi wey dey represent Rivers South East na correct and loyal ally. Wike talk this one during a Night of...

item.title: Young Nigerian Lecturer Shares Her Academic Journey and Career Advice
item.description: A 26-year-old Nigerian woman, Lisa Okoye, has become an inspiring figure for young people by securing a lecturing position at a university at a young age. Lisa joined university at 17 and earned two b...

item.title: US consumer sentiment falls sharply due to rising inflation concerns
item.description: In recent weeks, US consumer sentiment has fallen to a record low, reflecting growing concerns among Americans about rising prices amid the ongoing Iran conflict. Preliminary data for April shows the ...

item.title: Fuel Price Increase Push Brazil Inflation Higher in March Due to Global Energy Shock
item.description: Inflation for Brazil don rise to 4.14 percent year-on-year for March 2026, according to the official report wey the country statistics agency, IBGE, release. The increase na mainly because of the shar...

item.title: NASA's Artemis II Astronauts Prepare for Historic Splashdown After 10-Day Mission Around the Moon
item.description: The Artemis II mission is concluding after 10 days of orbiting the moon, with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen heading home for splashd...

item.title: Anya Confesses the Full Truth to Nadja in Midnight Phantom Chapter Ten
item.description: In the latest installment of the popular teleserye “Midnight Phantom,” Chapter Ten continues with a pivotal moment as Anya finally reveals the full truth to Nadja. The revelation brings a mix of emoti...

item.title: Fraudulent Border Wall Contract Cancelled in South Africa
item.description: A South African court has overturned a contract worth R86 million for the construction of a border wall after finding evidence of fraud and irregularities. The ruling highlights a concerning trend of ...
